title: Home Intrusion (Victim Inside)
keywords: help me someone is in here
I am hiding in the closet
someone is trying to get in my room
steps: Move to a safe room (bedroom/bathroom) and lock the door.
Call 112/911 immediately and keep the line open.
Stay silent and turn off all lights.
Do not confront the intruder unless necessary for self-defense.
Stay on the line until police confirm they are on site.

title: Home Intrusion (Witness / Neighbor)
keywords: I see someone in my neighbor's house
masked man at the window next door
someone is breaking into the house across the street
I see a flashlight in the house next door
steps: Stay in a safe location away from the intrusion.
Call 112/911 and report the address of the break-in.
Note the description of the intruder: clothing, height, direction of entry.
Identify any suspicious vehicles: make, model, color, or license plate.
Do not attempt to intervene or shout at the intruder.

title: Active Shooter (Victim / Trapped)
keywords: I hear gunshots in the building
the shooter is in the hallway
I am bleeding from a gunshot
steps: RUN: Escape if there is a clear path. Leave belongings. Keep hands visible.
HIDE: If escape is impossible, lock and barricade the door. Silence your phone.
FIGHT: As a absolute last resort, act with maximum physical aggression to incapacitate the shooter.
Wait for police. Follow instructions with hands empty and visible.

title: Active Shooter (Witness / Caller)
keywords: I see a man with a gun
man with a rifle in public
he is shooting at the crowd
I hear shots at the mall
steps: Call 112/911 from a secure location.
Identify location: building name, floor, room number.
Report number of shooters and physical description (clothing, height).
Report type of weapons (handgun, rifle) and direction of travel.
Direct others away from the danger zone if safe to do so.

title: Suspicious Package / Bomb Threat
steps: Do NOT touch, move, or open the package.
Evacuate the immediate area and prevent others from entering.
Do NOT use two-way radios or cell phones within 50 feet (15 meters) of the object.
Call emergency services from a safe distance outside the building.
Be prepared to provide a description: size, shape, markings, and any sounds (ticking) or smells.
Follow evacuation routes and stay away from windows or glass partitions.

title: Abduction / Kidnapping Attempt
steps: If someone attempts to force you into a vehicle, fight back aggressively and yell for help.
Draw as much attention as possible to the situation.
If you are abducted, try to leave markers (scent, clothing, objects) for search teams.
Note descriptions of the abductor: age, height, marks/tattoos, and vehicle details.
If safe, use your phone to send your 'Live Location' to trust contacts immediately.
Stay as calm as possible and look for any opportunity to escape safely.

title: Animal Attack (Domestic / Wild)
steps: DOG: Stay still, do not make eye contact. If it attacks, use a jacket or bag as a shield.
BEAR: Speak calmly and back away slowly without turning your back. If it's a grizzly, play dead; if black bear, fight back.
SNAKE: Back away slowly; do not attempt to kill it. If bitten, stay calm and keep the limb below heart level.
WILD CAT (Cougar/Lion): Stand tall, look large, and do NOT run (triggers chase instinct).
Seek immediate medical attention for any bite or scratch (risk of rabies/infection).

title: Identity Theft / Digital Security Breach
steps: If you suspect identity theft, freeze your credit reports immediately with major bureaus.
Change all passwords for sensitive accounts (banking, email, social media) using a safe device.
Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on all accounts that support it.
Notify your bank and credit card issuers to flag fraudulent transactions.
File a report with the police or your national identity theft agency.
Monitor your financial statements and credit reports closely for at least 12 months.

title: Civil Unrest / Riot Survival
steps: If you see a large, agitated crowd forming, leave the area immediately.
Avoid wearing clothes that make you look like a participant or a specific target.
If caught in a crowd, stay on the periphery. Do not move against the flow of people.
Avoid glass storefronts and entranceways; stay close to sturdy walls.
If you are indoors, stay away from windows and lock all doors. Turn off lights if the building is a target.
Do not stop to watch or take photos; your priority is moving to a safe, quiet location.

title: Home Invasion (Occupied)
steps: If someone breaks in while you are home, your priority is escape. Use a window or back door if possible.
If you cannot escape, LOCK yourself in a 'safe room' (like a bedroom or bathroom) and barricade the door.
Call emergency services immediately and stay on the line. Speak in a whisper.
If confronted, do not resist if the intruder only wants property. Give them what they ask for.
Avoid making eye contact or sudden movements that could be interpreted as a threat.
Try to remember specific details about the intruder for the police report.

title: Vehicle Carjacking (Victim / Post-Robbery)
keywords: at a red light they took my car
my car was stolen at gunpoint
steps: Move to a safe location immediately. Do not attempt to chase the vehicle.
Call 112/911. Report the time, location, and direction of travel.
Provide vehicle details: Make, model, color, and license plate number.
If you have a GPS tracking service (OnStar, etc.), notify them and the police.
Contact your insurance company to report the theft after filing the police report.

title: Vehicle Carjacking (Witness / Reporting)
keywords: someone being forced out of car
witnessed a man being pulled out of his car
reporting an auto theft in progress
steps: Stay safe and do not intervene. Note the suspect's description and weapons.
Call 112/911. Identify yourself as a witness.
Report the license plate and vehicle description of the stolen car.
Identify any accomplice vehicles used by the thieves.
Wait for police to arrive to provide a full statement if safe to do so.

title: Stalking / Persistent Harassment
steps: Trust your instincts. If you feel you are being followed, go to a well-lit, public place immediately.
Do NOT go home if you are being followed. Drive to a police station or fire station.
Keep a detailed log of all incidents, including dates, times, locations, and descriptions.
Inform family, friends, and coworkers about the situation so they can watch for suspicious activity.
Enhance your home security (cameras, locks) and vary your daily routes and routines.
Seek a protection order or restraining order through local law enforcement.

title: ATM / Cash Withdrawal Security
steps: Use well-lit, busy ATMs. Avoid using them late at night or in isolated areas.
Check the ATM for skimmers or loose parts before inserting your card.
Shield the keypad with your hand while entering your PIN.
If you are approached by someone after a withdrawal, do not count your money; put it away and leave immediately.
If someone attempts to rob you, comply. Your cash is replaceable, your life is not.
Call the bank and the police immediately if your card is stolen or you suspect fraudulent activity.

title: Workplace Violence / Suspicious Colleague
steps: If a colleague makes direct or indirect threats, report it to HR or Security immediately. Do NOT ignore it.
Watch for 'Red Flags': extreme agitation, mentions of weapons, or sudden changes in behavior.
If an incident occurs, move to a safe room with a lock and stay quiet.
Avoid escalating the situation by arguing or confronting the aggressive individual alone.
Familiarize yourself with your company's emergency response plan and exit routes.
Call emergency services if you believe there is an immediate threat to life or safety.

title: Street Robbery / Mugging (Victim / Post-Incident)
keywords: I'm safe now but I was robbed
steps: Find a safe, well-lit location with other people immediately.
Call 112/911. State your location and if you are injured.
Note the suspect's description: clothing, height, age, and direction of flight.
Block your credit cards and phone remotely via a safe device.
Stay calm. Do not touch anything the attacker might have discarded.

title: Street Robbery / Mugging (Witness / Reporting)
keywords: someone is being held at knifepoint
I see a thief running with a bag
steps: Do not intervene if the attacker is armed. Keep a safe distance.
Call 112/911 immediately. Report the exact location and suspect's direction.
Provide a description of the suspect: gender, clothing, hair, weapons seen.
Assist the victim only once the attacker has completely left the area.
Stay on site to provide a statement to arriving officers.

title: Domestic Violence / Immediate Threat
steps: If you are in immediate danger, try to get to a room with a lock and a phone.
Call emergency services (112/911) immediately. If you can't speak, leave the line open.
Try to get out of the house or to a safe neighbor's house.
Avoid the kitchen (knives) and small, enclosed spaces with no exits.
If you have a 'safe word' with friends or family, send it now.
Listen to your instincts; if you think the situation is escalating, prioritize escape over possessions.

title: Public Transport Attack / Hostage Situation
steps: Stay calm and avoid eye contact with the attacker or hostage-taker.
Follow instructions from the attacker unless it involves moving to a secondary, more dangerous location.
If you are on a train or bus, stay low to the floor to avoid crossfire.
If safe, use your phone to send a silent text or use an emergency app to signal your location.
Observe details about the attackers: number of people, weapons, and any names or specific demands mentioned.
Do NOT attempt to be a hero unless you have a clear, immediate opportunity to save lives with minimal risk.

title: Sexual Assault / Immediate Threat
steps: Get to a safe, well-lit place with other people as quickly as possible.
Call emergency services immediately.
Do NOT shower, wash your hands, change clothes, or clean the area where the assault occurred (this preserves critical DNA evidence).
Seek medical attention immediately at a hospital that can perform a forensic exam (rape kit).
If you have physical injuries, do not touch or clean them until they are documented by medical professionals.
Contact a local sexual assault support center for counseling and legal guidance.

title: Hate Crime / Targeted Attack
steps: Your priority is physical safety; move away from the aggressor immediately.
Call emergency services and clearly state that you are being targeted because of your identity (race, religion, etc.).
If there are witnesses, ask them to stay and provide a statement or get their contact information.
Keep any physical evidence, such as biased notes, recorded audio/video, or photos of property damage.
Report the incident as a hate crime to local law enforcement and your national civil rights agency.
Document specific slurs or biased language used by the attacker.

title: Cyberstalking / Doxing / Swatting
steps: If your private information (address, phone) is leaked online, contact the platform to request immediate removal.
If you are threatened with 'swatting', contact your local police department non-emergency line to alert them of the potential false report.
Document all threats: take screenshots of messages, profiles, and timestamps.
Set all social media accounts to private and change passwords using a secure password manager.
Notify your employer or school if the harassment could affect your physical safety at those locations.
Report cyberstalking to the FBI's Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3) or your national equivalent.

title: Prowler / Suspicious Person (Victim Inside)
steps: Do NOT confront the person. Stay inside and lock all doors and windows.
Move to a central room away from windows.
Call 112/911 and provide a description of the person and their location.
Keep the lights on to show the house is occupied, or turn them off to hide, depending on your safety.

title: Domestic Violence (Witness / Reporting)
steps: Do NOT intervene directly if you believe it is dangerous.
Call 112/911. Provide the exact address and apartment number if known.
Inform the dispatcher if you hear children involved or weapons mentioned.
Note the time and nature of the sounds (screams, breaking objects, threats).
Stay in a safe location until officers arrive to provide your statement.

title: Vandalism / Graffiti / Property Damage
steps: Maintain a safe distance and do NOT confront the vandals.
Call emergency services or the non-emergency line to report the location.
If safe, take photos or video of the individuals and any vehicles involved.
Note the description of the vandals: clothing, approximate age, and direction of travel.
Do NOT attempt to clean or repair the damage until police have documented it.
